_meta.description: Catalog of ALL dome model predictions — confirmed (WINs), prospective, pending, falsified, refined. This is our tracking ledger, not the dome's. Every prediction the dome registers gets an entry here. Counts are computed at build time and injected into HTML via {{PLACEHOLDER}} tokens.

pipeline.poller: Detects new predictions on dome site (hard rule: >=5 new = critical). Logs to change records.
pipeline.analyst: Mode 0 expanded: catalogs new predictions here with testability assessment. One entry per prediction.
pipeline.decider: Triages analyst entries, updates outcomes when test windows close, routes to curmudgeon for review.
pipeline.curmudgeon: Reviews testability assessments and derivation claims.
pipeline.build: generate-html.js reads this file, computes counts, injects into HTML via placeholders.

placeholders.{{PRED_PROSPECTIVE}}: Predictions with cryptographic timestamps registered before data pull
placeholders.{{PRED_PENDING}}: Predictions awaiting test window or data
placeholders.{{PRED_CONFIRMED}}: Predictions confirmed by data (overlap with wins.json WINs)
placeholders.{{PRED_FALSIFIED}}: Predictions falsified by data (overlap with uncounted-failures.json)
placeholders.{{PRED_EXPIRED}}: Test window closed with no data or ambiguous result
placeholders.{{PRED_STD_RELABEL}}: Predictions that are standard physics relabeled (our assessment)
placeholders.{{PRED_TESTABLE}}: Predictions with specific, measurable test criteria
placeholders.{{PRED_DOME_DERIVED}}: Predictions genuinely derived from dome geometry (our assessment)
placeholders.{{PRED_ACTIVE_WINDOWS}}: Predictions with test windows open right now
placeholders.{{PRED_RECYCLED}}: Predictions that restate already-confirmed WINs with new timestamps

recycling_detection.restates_win: WIN ID (e.g. 'WIN-068') if this prediction restates an already-confirmed WIN, null otherwise. This is the primary recycling indicator.
recycling_detection.data_predates_registration: Boolean. True if the underlying observational data was published or available before the dome's registration date. Distinguishes genuinely prospective predictions from timestamped retrodictions.
recycling_detection.crypto_hash: SHA-256 hex string from dome's registration (if provided). Hash formula claimed: SHA256('ECM V51.0 {ID}: {text} on {date}T00:00:00Z'). No independent verification mechanism exists.
recycling_detection.registration_date: ISO date when dome registered the prediction (from git commit or claimed date)

summary.notes: Complete catalog of ALL dome predictions from john09289.github.io/predictions/. Includes recycled predictions (restating WINs), logging-only items, falsified, and pending. 86 entries cataloged.

WIN-068 was verdicted 'Std Model Explains' because the Chap]

This is the most specific of the eclipse predictions — named stations, a defined amplitude range, and a clear success ]

[Assessment: This is a genuinely prospective prediction with a specific quantitative claim (10–13 µGal gravity pulse at eclipse contact phases in Level-3 SG residuals) and a closing test window (Aug 2026 eclipse).]

This is genuinely testable: ELF monitoring stations (e.g., AWO, Hylaty) continuously record Schumann frequencies, and]

entries.claim: SAA separation ≥57° by 2030
entries.notes:
SAA African and South Atlantic cells. Requires IGRF model tracking.
[2026-04-10 decider] Assessment integrated: Genuinely prospective with a long test window (through 2030). The SAA IS splitting per NASA and ESA Swarm data — outcome uncertain. Legitimate open prediction.

entries.claim: SAA African cell ≤21,750 nT by 2028
entries.notes: SAA intensity threshold. Extrapolation of known SAA weakening trend. [Assessment: WIN-035 already covers this exact claim: the SAA African cell falling below a specific nT threshold. We rated WIN-035 as Std Model Explains because WMM2025 and IGRF-14 both project continued SAA weake]

Restates WIN-043 NMP drift pattern. Extrapolates historical drift.
[2026-04-10 decider] Assessment integrated: Restates WIN-043 (NMP drift 2.26x longitudinal). Predicts NMP will reach 141-146°E by 2031 — extrapolation of already-observed drift framed as new prediction.

entries.claim: NMP drift ratio ≥2.0× through 2028
entries.notes:
Restates WIN-043 NMP rate acceleration. Based on observed trend.
[2026-04-10 decider] Assessment integrated: Also restates WIN-043 (NMP drift). Predicts NMP drift ratio >=2.0x through 2028 — same longitudinal drift already captured in WIN-043.

entries.claim: NMP position 85.756°N, 136.557°E by early 2027
entries.notes: Specific NMP coordinates. Testable against IGRF annual models. [Assessment: The North Magnetic Pole has been drifting toward Siberia along a well-characterized trajectory documented by NOAA NP.xy data since 1590. WMM2025 (released Dec 2024, 14 months before this prediction wa] [Curmudgeon note: the dome places the NMP at 85.756°N for 2027 — slightly south of the WMM2025 2025.0 position of ~86.0°N. The NMP has been drifting poleward; predicting it moves 0.24° equatorward by 2027 is against the recent trend. This is the one potentially discriminating aspect of this prediction — if confirmed it would suggest a deceleration/reversal the linear WMM extrapolation misses; if falsified it is an easy failure point.]

entries.claim: SAA separation 52–56° by December 2026
entries.notes: SAA cell separation by year-end. Mid-term test window. [Assessment: The SAA two-cell separation is a well-documented, steadily increasing trend driven by reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary (Terra-Nova et al. 2017). WIN-004 already covers exponential SAA]

entries.claim: TTB ≥35 nT decay January-June 2026
entries.notes: Triple-component baseline (TTB) decay rate. IMMINENT — test window closes 2026-06-30. [Assessment: This is a genuinely prospective, time-bound prediction: TTB (triple-component baseline) decay of ≥35 nT between January-June 2026. The test window closes 2026-06-30 — IMMINENT. The prediction is speci]

entries.claim: Schumann resonance ≥7.85 Hz during solar wind >5 nPa
entries.notes:
Weekly test. Data exists; outcome unresolved (conflicting reports).
[2026-04-10 decider] Assessment integrated: Schumann resonance frequency modulation by solar wind dynamic pressure is well-documented ionospheric physics. Not a novel ECM prediction.

entries.claim: hmF2 descent ≥10 km after solar wind >6 nPa
entries.notes:
hmF2 (ionospheric peak height) response test. Unresolved.
[2026-04-10 decider] Assessment integrated: hmF2 (F2-layer peak height) descent during high solar wind dynamic pressure is textbook space weather. Standard ionospheric physics, not ECM-specific.

entries.claim: NMP drift poleward dominates over equatorward drift
entries.notes: NMP drift direction test. REFINED — lateral drift dominates, not poleward. [Decider 2026-04-11: Changed from standard_physics to falsified. Curmudgeon review: the prediction specifically claimed poleward drift DOMINATES over lateral. Author REFINED status concedes lateral drift is 5.5x larger than poleward — the directional claim was contradicted by observations. This is falsification of the specific directional prediction, not merely a non-discriminating test.]

entries.claim: Roaring 40s 500 hPa anomaly ≥3%
entries.notes: Roaring 40s geopotential height anomaly. REFINED — smaller effect than predicted. [Decider 2026-04-11: Changed from unfalsifiable to falsified. Curmudgeon review: the prediction specified a concrete ≥3% threshold. Author REFINED status concedes the observed effect is smaller. A prediction with a specific quantitative criterion that falls short of that criterion is falsified, not unfalsifiable. Author defined the success criterion (≥3%) and observations did not meet it.]

entries.claim: Moon angular size >2% monthly variation
entries.notes:
Moon apparent size variation. SUSPENDED — measurement methodology disputed.
[2026-04-10 decider] Assessment integrated: Claims >2% monthly variation in Moon angular size. This is standard celestial mechanics: the Moon orbital eccentricity causes ~14% variation in apparent diameter over anomalistic month.

entries.claim: Aetheric gravity wave speed 9.2 km/s
entries.notes: Proposed wave speed. Unfalsifiable without mechanism definition. [Decider 2026-04-11: Changed from unfalsifiable to standard_physics. Curmudgeon review: 9.2 km/s sits squarely within known P-wave velocities in the upper mantle (8-13 km/s). This is relabeled seismology — the claim HAS been tested and matches standard geophysics. standard_physics is more accurate than unfalsifiable because: (a) the test was done and trivially passes under standard physics, and (b) calling it unfalsifiable gives the dome an unearned grievance when reality is worse for them — it matches standard seismology exactly, not novel dome predictions.]

entries.claim: Deep earthquake P-wave shadow zone at 104–140°
entries.notes:
Shadow zone claim. Standard seismology. Test window expired. RESTATES standard model.
[2026-04-10 decider] Assessment integrated: The P-wave shadow zone at 104–140° angular distance is one of the most well-established results in seismology, known since Oldham (1906). Standard physics prediction falsely claimed as ECM success.

entries.claim: S-wave shadow zone at 120–160°
entries.notes: S-wave shadow standard seismology. No discriminating test. IMMINENT.

entries.claim: M2 tidal amplitude <0.5% variation at Honolulu
entries.notes:
M2 tidal stability claim. Testable against UHSLC data. IMMINENT.
[2026-04-10 decider] Assessment integrated: M2 tidal amplitude stability at Honolulu is a trivially predictable result from standard tidal theory. NOAA tidal gauge records show M2 amplitude varies <5% over solar cycle. No dome-specific prediction.

entries.claim: Lunar perigee tidal range 12% higher
entries.notes: Lunar distance tidal modulation. Expected by tidal theory. [Assessment: Lunar perigee tidal range being ~12% higher than average is a textbook prediction of Newtonian tidal theory. The gravitational tidal force scales as 1/r³; the Moon's distance varies ±5.5% from mean (p] [Curmudgeon correction: 1/r³ scaling gives ~25% force increase at perigee (r_mean=384400 km, r_perigee=356500 km: ratio³=1.254), not ~17%. Observed coastal range increase of ~12% reflects bathymetric damping of the raw 25% force increase. Verdict standard_physics confirmed.]

entries.claim: Northern hemisphere TOA flux excess >0.3 W/m²
entries.notes: Restates WIN-066 heat excess trend. Data predates registration. IMMINENT. EXP-063: Assessed recycled — Raghuraman et al. (2025 PNAS) published before April 2026 registration date. Dome conflates decadal trend rate (W/m²/decade) with static hemispheric excess (>0.3 W/m²). Standard physics (aerosol changes, ice-albedo feedback) fully explains asymmetry. Dome disc geometry predicts ~90–270 W/m² asymmetry by inverse-square law — orders of magnitude larger than observed.

entries.claim: Northern hemisphere heat excess +0.36 W/m² by 2028
entries.notes: Heat accumulation extrapolation. Standard climate metric. [Decider 2026-04-11: Changed from standard_physics to recycled. Curmudgeon review: This prediction substantively restates WIN-066 (NH heat excess asymmetry). Assessment explicitly identifies the overlap. Consistent with how PRED-094, PRED-099, PRED-100 are handled — all WIN-restating predictions should be recycled, not standard_physics.]

entries.claim: JWST z<0.1 Type Ia supernovae show no acceleration

[2026-04-10 decider] Assessment integrated: JWST z<0.1 Type Ia supernovae show no acceleration at z<0.1 is standard cosmology — deceleration was not expected at such low redshifts. This is textbook standard candle behavior, not an ECM prediction.

entries.claim: HER/TTB field component decay >40 nT/year through 2027
entries.notes: Geomagnetic component decay rate. Testable against observatory data. [Assessment: Hermanus (HER) and Tsumeb (TTB) are INTERMAGNET observatories within the SAA region. The SAA African cell has been decaying at ~28–35 nT/year at its core for decades, well-documented by WMM and IGRF m] [Curmudgeon note: unlike typical SAA predictions (WIN-037/042 which set ≥28 nT, the lower bound of the documented range), this prediction sets >40 nT/yr — 14–43% above the historical 28–35 nT/yr typical range. This is the most falsifiable geomagnetic prediction in the catalog. If decay remains at 28–35 nT/yr through 2027, this prediction fails. CMB flux-patch dynamics allow localized acceleration so standard_physics verdict stands, but confirmation here would be more discriminating than for other SAA predictions.]
